message:
fix(http): properly skip 1xx informational responses in HTTP/1.1 client

RFC 9110 Section 15.2: Informational 1xx responses (100 Continue,
103 Early Hints, etc.) are interim—the client must continue reading
until a final (2xx-5xx) response arrives. Exception: 101 Switching
Protocols is final (it changes the connection semantics entirely).

Previously, the client treated all 1xx responses the same as final
responses in the no_body_status match, which meant a 100 Continue
response would be returned to the caller as the final response,
and the actual 200 OK would never be read. This broke any upload
flow that relied on Expect: 100-continue, as well as servers that
send 103 Early Hints for preloading.

Changes:
  - Add a loop that re-reads response headers when status is 1xx
    (except 101), consuming interim responses transparently
  - Adjust no_body_status to use .contains() for the 1xx range
    (covering the edge case where 101 Switching Protocols still
    reports as no-body)

Tests:
  - request_streaming_skips_informational_response: 100 Continue
    followed by 200 OK with body "hello"
  - request_skips_informational_response: 103 Early Hints with
    Link header followed by 200 OK with body "ok"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
